corpus.title: Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa
corpus.signal_text:
Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa

Emergency crews battled fires and searched damaged buildings across Ukraine on Tuesday 15 July after Russian strikes on the cities of Sumy and Odesa killed six people and injured at least 20 others, according to regional authorities.

corpus.title: Russia Shifts Its Focus to the Black Sea
corpus.signal_text:
Russia Shifts Its Focus to the Black Sea

Moscow is targeting trade routes and deepwater ports to strain Ukraine’s wartime economy.

corpus.title: Ukraine Says It Hit 159 Russian Shadow Fleet Vessels in 12 Days
corpus.signal_text:
Ukraine’s Unmanned Systems Forces (USF) have struck 159 vessels linked to Russia’s so-called shadow fleet in just 12 days, including 12 vessels hit overnight, according to USF Commander Robert “Madyar” Brovdi.
In a post on Telegram on Thursday, Brovdi said the campaign, dubbed Operation “Molochka” (Dairy), continues to target Russian maritime logistics used to transport oil, fuel, and cargo in circumvention of international sanctions.
JOIN US ON TELEGRAM
Follow our coverage of the war on the @Kyivpost_official.
“FLEETDROP continues: 159 vessels in 12 days of Operation ‘MoLoChKa.’ +12 shadow fleet vessels hit by USF Birds in the Black Sea on July 17: nine dry cargo ships, one tanker, one gas carrier, and one tugboat,” Brovdi wrote.
According to him, Ukrainian forces struck 159 vessels between July 6 and July 17, including 117 in the Sea of Azov and 42 in the Black Sea.
He said the operation aims to cripple Russia’s maritime logistics without causing environmental disasters.
“The goal is the irreversible paralysis of oil, fuel, and cargo logistics used to bypass sanctions. We want every self-propelled vessel to become a drifting barge – blind and deaf. The objective is not to pollute the sea with oil spills, so we are not sinking them,” he wrote.
Brovdi added that evidence of the reported strikes would be released later.
Earlier, USF commander said the operation is designed to cripple Russia’s feeder fleet – small and medium-sized flat-bottomed tankers, typically about 140 meters (460 feet) long with a deadweight of around 7,000 tons.
A ‘Gift’ to Moscow: Russian War Bloggers Cheer Dismissal of Ukraine’s Tech Chief
According to him, these vessels are a critical part of Russia’s so-called shadow fleet, transporting oil through the Volga-Don Canal and the Sea of Azov from Russian ports to larger tankers waiting in the Black Sea.
Because deep-draft tankers cannot enter the shallow ports, oil must first be transferred offshore by feeder vessels. Brovdi said a single large tanker is typically loaded by 12 to 15 feeder tankers.
“The burning of these wormy (Russian) camel tankers – and the tugboats that stubbornly tow them after they are damaged – also limits deliveries of scarce gasoline to Crimea through the narrow, shallow waters of the Sea of Azov,” he wrote.
Kyiv has defended the campaign, arguing that Russia’s shadow fleet finances the Kremlin’s war effort and therefore constitutes a legitimate military target.

corpus.title: 2 Killed, 6 Wounded After Russian Ballistic Missiles Strike Kyiv Overnight
corpus.signal_text:
2 Killed, 6 Wounded After Russian Ballistic Missiles Strike Kyiv Overnight

Russian ballistic missiles targeted Kyiv overnight into July 16, striking warehouse and administrative buildings in the Darnytskyi and Sviatoshynskyi districts. Two people were killed, while at least five others were injured, including a teenager.

corpus.title: Russia says it returned the bodies of 501 Ukrainian soldiers and received 31 in exchange
corpus.signal_text:
Russia says it returned the bodies of 501 Ukrainian soldiers and received 31 in exchange

Russia and Ukraine again exchanged the bodies of fallen soldiers, State Duma deputy Shamsail Saraliyev told the business news outlet RBC. Russia handed over 501 bodies to Ukraine and received 31 in exchange, Saraliyev said. Ukraine had not confirmed the exchange at the time of publication.

corpus.title: Russia strikes Kyiv for the second time in a week, killing two and wounding six
corpus.signal_text:
Russia strikes Kyiv for the second time in a week, killing two and wounding six

Russian forces struck Kyiv with missiles in the early hours of July 16, the head of the city’s military administration, Tymur Tkachenko, reported. The attack killed two people and wounded six others, including a 16-year-old, Mayor Vitaliy Klitschko said.

corpus.title: Russia and Ukraine Exchange Bodies of Over 530 Fallen Soldiers
corpus.signal_text:
Russia and Ukraine Exchange Bodies of Over 530 Fallen Soldiers

Ukraine received 501 bodies of its soldiers while handing Russia the remains of 31 soldiers, Shamsail Saraliyev, a State Duma lawmaker, told RBC.

corpus.title: Russia launches deadly barrage on Kyiv region on eve of NATO summit
corpus.signal_text:
Russia launches deadly barrage on Kyiv region on eve of NATO summit

Russia struck Ukraine's Kyiv region with ballistic missiles and drones on Monday, killing at least 11 people and wounding dozens, authorities said, on the eve of a NATO summit in Turkey.

corpus.title: Russian and Ukrainian attacks kill at least 13, officials say
corpus.signal_text:
Russian and Ukrainian attacks kill at least 13, officials say

KYIV, July 16 - Russian and Ukrainian attacks on civilian areas in towns and cities, many on the front line of the more than four-year-old war, killed at least 13 people on Thursday, local officials said.

corpus.title: One woman killed and six others injured in Russian drone attack on Kherson
corpus.signal_text:
One woman killed and six others injured in Russian drone attack on Kherson
One person was killed and six others were injured in a Russian FPV drone attack on the Dniprovskyi district of the city of Kherson on the morning of 17 July.
Source: Yaroslav Shanko, Head of Kherson City Military Administration; Oleksandr Prokudin, Head of Kherson Oblast Military Administration
Quote from Prokudin: "In the Dniprovskyi district... at around 09:30, a 70-year-old woman, a Kherson resident, was killed in an enemy FPV drone attack."
Details: Shanko reported that six more people were injured in the attack.
A 61-year-old man and four women aged 71, 73, 75 and 85 sustained blast injuries and shrapnel wounds. They are currently in hospital.
A 48-year-old Kherson resident suffered concussion, a blast injury, a closed traumatic brain injury and a shrapnel wound to the chest. His condition is severe.
The injured have been hospitalised.
Background: Russian forces attacked Kherson on the night of 16-17 July and on the morning of 17 July, killing one person and injuring another in those strikes.

corpus.title: Russia drops five guided aerial bombs on Sumy, civilian infrastructure hit
corpus.signal_text:
Russia drops five guided aerial bombs on Sumy, civilian infrastructure hit

Russian forces carried out five guided aerial bomb strikes on Sumy overnight, damaging civilian infrastructure facilities. The authorities are assessing casualties and the extent of the damage.

corpus.title: Air Defense Forces shoot down five Russian missiles and 115 drones
corpus.signal_text:
Air Defense Forces shoot down five Russian missiles and 115 drones

Air Defense Forces have shot down five guided aerial bombs and 115 drones used by Russian troops to attack Ukraine since the evening of July 16. Ten enemy weapons hit multiple locations.

corpus.title: Russian forces launch 853 strikes on Zaporizhzhia region over past 24 hours, killing three
corpus.signal_text:
Russian forces launch 853 strikes on Zaporizhzhia region over past 24 hours, killing three

Over the past 24 hours, the Russian army carried out 853 strikes on 58 settlements in the Zaporizhzhia region, killing three people and wounding five others.
